title: The Date Has a Time Zone
subtitle: Backrooms now has a cleaner July 14 home-release target, while Prime Video's July 13 timestamp explains the remaining one-day wobble.

lead.title: The date finally stops sliding, mostly
lead.summary: Edition 002 left Backrooms in a pre-order corridor: A24 had opened storefront links, Prime Video showed a July 13 evening timestamp, and the earlier July 7 report had clearly failed as a launch date. Edition 003 narrows the record. July 10 home-entertainment reporting now points to Tuesday, July 14 as the ordinary digital rent-or-buy date, while Prime's July 13 9:00 PM PDT listing explains why some pages still appear one calendar day earlier.

briefings.title: July 14 becomes the clean viewer-facing date
briefings.summary: Screen-Connections, Bloody Disgusting, and /Film all reported July 14 as the digital rent-or-buy date. The A24 page continued to expose official pre-order links, and Prime Video continued to list July 13, 2026 at 9:00 PM PDT.
briefings.analysis: For U.S. viewers, this resolves the apparent contradiction better than creating a new one: July 13 at 9 PM Pacific is July 14 in much of the country and matches the Tuesday home-video reporting. The archive should still wait for actual availability before calling the release complete.

briefings.title: The bonus-footage question survives the date
briefings.summary: Reviewed storefront and home-entertainment sources identify the standard home-release path, but none of them say the Everything Must Go post-credit footage is included in the digital version.
briefings.analysis: That makes Edition 003 narrower than a victory lap. The digital date is stronger; the version question is not. Until a storefront lists an extended edition, bonus item, runtime, or extras package, the theatrical footage remains outside the proven home-viewing scope.

briefings.title: The July 7 miss is now historical context
briefings.summary: The earlier July 7 PVOD expectation remains useful because it explains why the release file looked unstable. Later evidence now points away from July 7 and toward July 14.
briefings.analysis: The correct editorial move is not to erase the miss. Edition 003 keeps it as the audit trail: AP reported July 7, Decider documented the failure to appear that day, A24 opened pre-orders after that, and the date then consolidated around July 14.

briefings.title: No new official YouTube-side signal surfaced
briefings.summary: A fresh official Kane Pixels channel surface check did not expose a new canonical People Still Live Here or Backrooms-series upload beyond the already tracked visible titles.
briefings.analysis: This is included as a negative finding because Edition 001 and Edition 002 both kept YouTube-side continuity on watch. No new official upload should be inferred from chatter; the next meaningful update still needs to come from the channel, playlist metadata, or a primary creator statement.

watchList.title: July 14 availability
watchList.text: Check Apple TV, Prime Video, Fandango at Home, and A24 on July 14 before describing Backrooms as actually available to rent or buy.

watchList.title: Version and runtime
watchList.text: Capture runtime, extras, and edition labels once the digital version is playable. Do not infer Everything Must Go inclusion from a generic Backrooms listing.

watchList.title: YouTube-side continuity
watchList.text: Keep People Still Live Here and official Kane Pixels uploads on watch, but treat the July 11 channel check as no new canonical update.

editorialNote: Edition 003 is in review until the user approves publication. Before publishing, recheck whether the July 14 digital listing has changed or whether a storefront identifies the Everything Must Go footage.
